page.title: NCM формат 3D-активов on-chain
page.description: NCM is NiceChunk's compact Solana game asset format. Store voxel 3D models as PDA-friendly chain text without IPFS.

hero.title: We designed NCM so Solana games can own their 3D assets on-chain.
hero.body: NCM is NiceChunk's compact 3D asset format for Solana and other chains. We are building deeper R&D around this format because it can compress voxel assets into tiny text records that clients fetch directly through RPC, verify publicly, and keep available without IPFS.
hero.formatBody: Palette, box count, signed positions, and dimensions encoded as compact varints.

rpc.title: RPC native
rpc.body: A client can read the text from account data and reconstruct the model immediately.

public.title: Public forever
public.body: The asset is stored with the program state, not hidden behind a mutable external gateway.

compact.title: Cost aware
compact.body: Same-color voxels are merged into boxes before encoding to reduce storage size.

portable.title: Chain portable
portable.body: NCM is plain text, so the same asset format can be used beyond one runtime.

thesis.title: A Solana game asset should be part of the state, not a URL attached to it.
thesis.body: NiceChunk is building around NCM because a voxel object can be small enough to live directly inside a program-owned PDA. The asset, its rules, and its ownership can move together, so a game item or NFT does not depend on an external gateway staying online.

pda.title: PDA-native resources
pda.body: NCM turns small characters, props, equipment, and block decorations into account data that Solana clients can read through normal RPC calls.

trade.title: Safer NFT trading
trade.body: When the visual asset is stored with the asset record, buyers do not need to trust that an IPFS gateway, metadata server, or mutable JSON link will keep resolving later.

research.title: Useful compression research
research.body: NCM is also the base for our NCM-DNA exploration: searching for shorter deterministic genes that reproduce the same model and can turn asset compression into useful computation.

dna.title: View the NCM-DNA experiment beside the format it builds on.
dna.body: NCM-DNA continues the same direction: instead of only storing compact cuboids, it searches for deterministic genes that can reproduce the same model with fewer bytes.

converter.title: Локальный конвертер в браузере
converter.body: Upload a MagicaVoxel .vox file. The file is parsed locally in your browser and converted into NCM text.
converter.dropBody: MagicaVoxel SIZE, XYZI, and RGBA chunks are supported.

license.title: Sample attribution
license.body: Sample VOX characters are from OpenGameArt, created by JoshMckay, licensed under CC-BY 4.0.

output.title: Compact chain text
output.body: Store this payload in account data or a program-owned asset record. Clients can decode it without IPFS.

page.title: Лаборатория NCM DNA
page.description: NCM DNA Lab ищет короткие детерминированные гены, из которых можно вырастить ту же каноническую кубоидную модель.

hero.title: Лаборатория NCM DNA
hero.body: NCM DNA Lab — экспериментальная система поиска генов для on-chain 3D-активов. Вместо хранения каждого куба она ищет более короткий детерминированный ген, который может вырастить ту же модель.
hero.pow: В будущем такой поиск может стать полезным Proof of Work: майнеры тратят CPU, находят более короткие гены, уменьшают размер PDA-хранилища и получают награды из сэкономленной стоимости хранения.

cpu.title: Почему сначала CPU?
cpu.body: NCM-DNA v1 использует основанный на правилах язык роста кубоидов, а не удобные для GPU воксельные функции. Поиск содержит ветвления, шаблоны, симметрию, каноническую сортировку и гены переменной длины, что лучше подходит для обычного CPU.

chain.title: Путь отправки с экономией хранения
chain.step1: PDA актива хранит canonical_model_hash и current_encoding.
chain.step3: Программа проверяет, что хэш grow(gene) равен canonical_model_hash.
chain.step4: Если ген короче, кодировка актива обновляется.
chain.step5: Освобожденная PDA-рента может делиться между майнером, владельцем и пулом протокола.

page.title: NCM Function Model Lab
page.description: A NiceChunk lab that loads VOX models, maps colors to a shared palette, emits compact on-chain function bytes, and redraws the model from that payload.

hero.title: Load a VOX model and redraw it from a function

tech.title: Technical idea
tech.body: The function payload stores only G0 palette indexes and box-basis bytes. Later miners can use the exact model hash as the target and search for a shorter Fourier gene.

steps.sample: Load VOX and canonicalize its voxel/color grid.
steps.fit: Map colors to the shared G0 palette and merge same-color voxels into box basis functions.
steps.rebuild: Evaluate the function bytes and redraw the model from G0 palette indexes.
steps.bake: Use the exact result hash as a future PoW target for shorter genes.

meta.title: NiceChunk Civilization Codex
meta.description: NiceChunk Civilization Codex: players write, read, sign, validate, and execute world rules without project-side privilege.

hero.title: Civilization Codex
hero.subtitle: Players do not only play the world. They write the laws of the world.
hero.body: NiceChunk civilization rules are designed for a public on-chain world where resources, equipment, privileges, and world parameters cannot appear from nowhere.

layers.title: A world constitution and an evolving civilization layer.
layers.body: Natural Law protects the world from arbitrary authority. Civilization Law lets players evolve production, construction, craft, and society without turning governance into capital voting.

natural.title: Natural Law
natural.subtitle: Constitution-level consensus for the deepest fairness rules: conservation, asset protection, world generation, NCK principles, and zero project-side privilege.

civilization.title: Civilization Law
civilization.subtitle: Open evolution for production, building, craft, resource processing, non-constitutional parameters, and society-level rules.

constitution.title: Natural Law is not a normal vote.
constitution.body: A Natural Law change must face stronger prompts, stricter validators, longer cooling, and broader challenge windows because it can affect the deepest fairness assumptions of the world.

checks.stricterValidation: Validator rejects privilege, inflation, and ownership seizure
checks.strongerPrompt: Players must explicitly confirm they understand the rule
checks.widerChallenge: Longer challenge window for auditors and citizens

power.title: Power comes from real participation, not wallet count or raw NCK balance.
power.body: The future contract should weight citizens by world participation while limiting abuse through maturity, caps, active windows, decay, and anomaly penalties.

sources.registration: Registered citizen state and mature world identity
sources.mining: Mined blocks with verifiable generated sources
sources.guardianService: Guardian service coverage and proof submission
sources.ruleReading: Reading and understanding rule books before signing

lifecycle.title: A rule book moves from writing to execution without project-side permission.

draft.title: Draft Rule Book
draft.body: A player writes readable intent and a machine patch for a target module.

publish.title: Publish On-chain Hash
publish.body: The book fixes text_hash, patch_hash, author, law type, risk level, and snapshot epoch.

read.title: Read & Understand
read.body: Citizens inspect the text, impact scope, old asset policy, and validator warnings.

sign.title: Sign Civilization Power
sign.body: Citizens sign agree, reject, or abstain with snapshot-based civilization power.

threshold.title: Reach Threshold
threshold.body: Civilization Law needs over 50%; Natural Law needs over 90% active civilization power.

cooling.title: Cooling & Challenge
cooling.body: The rule waits through cooling and challenge windows so mistakes can be disputed.

execute.title: Execute Rule
execute.body: Any player can call execute_rule when on-chain conditions are satisfied.

active.title: Rule Becomes Active
active.body: The program writes the approved config into rule registries and module config PDAs.

schema.title: Human-readable intent and machine-executable patch stay bound by hashes.
schema.body: The UI stores mock data locally today. Later these fields can map directly to rule_book_pda, rule_signature_pda, and rule_registry_pda account layouts.

fields.author: Wallet that published the rule book hash.
fields.category: Natural Law or Civilization Law threshold class.
fields.target_module: One or more configuration modules affected by the patch.
fields.human_readable_text: Readable explanation that players review before signing.
fields.machine_patch: Structured patch bytes consumed by the RuleValidator.
fields.snapshot_epoch: Epoch used to freeze signer power.
fields.status: Lifecycle state from draft to active.

modules.title: Civilization changes the configuration layer, not core code by text command.

smelting.title: Smelting
smelting.body: Fuel tiers, raw inputs, conservation checks, material outputs, and processing requirements.

forging.title: Forging
forging.body: Tool, equipment, and building recipes that consume validated material resources.

block_properties.title: Block Properties
block_properties.body: Hardness, drops, break requirements, hazard flags, and world interaction parameters.

resource_rules.title: Resource Rules
resource_rules.body: Generated resource sources, output logic, provenance requirements, and conservation boundaries.

element_rules.title: Element Rules
element_rules.body: Element profiles, material tendencies, and future chemistry or alloy constraints.

world_knowledge.title: World Knowledge
world_knowledge.body: Versioned public knowledge, civilization records, rule explanations, and audit notes.

examples.naturalTitle: Rules that protect the world itself.

natural.resourceConservation: Resources cannot be created without generated source, input burn, or validated transformation.
natural.assetProtection: Player-owned assets cannot be seized or reassigned without valid rule authority.
natural.publicWorldGeneration: World seed and generation principles cannot be quietly changed.
natural.noProjectPrivilege: No address receives hidden minting, bypass, or privileged production rights.
natural.transparentAuthority: Contract authorities and config owners must remain inspectable.
natural.nckPrinciples: NCK and core economic principles cannot be casually broken.

civilization.bronze: Copper + tin can unlock bronze through smelting and forging configs.
civilization.oreEnergy: A rule can increase energy requirements for a rare ore smelting path.
civilization.blockHardness: A new block can receive hardness, drops, and required tool tiers.
civilization.toolRecipe: A civilization can add a new tool forging recipe with material inputs.
civilization.materialProcessing: A material can require extra processing before construction use.

execution.title: Automatic execution means permissionless execution, not a server changing code.
execution.body: Once threshold, cooling, and challenge conditions are satisfied, any player can call execute_rule. The contract checks only on-chain state and writes the approved configuration into PDA registries.

ruleValidator.title: RuleValidator gate
ruleValidator.body: Rejects patches that create resources from nothing, bypass inputs, grant address privilege, or seize owned assets.

registryWrite.title: Registry write
registryWrite.body: Approved patches update rule_registry, smelting_config, forging_config, block_property_config, or resource_config PDAs.

permissionlessCaller.title: No privileged executor
permissionlessCaller.body: Execution has no project-side allowlist; any player can submit the transaction when conditions pass.

fixedCoreContracts.title: Fixed core contracts
fixedCoreContracts.body: Gameplay contracts should read active config PDAs instead of letting text rules upgrade program code.

mock.title: Local data today, chain accounts tomorrow.
mock.body: These examples are intentionally structured like future on-chain data so wallet signing, PDA reads, and execute_rule flows can replace the mock layer later.

contract.title: The civilization program should mutate rule configuration PDAs through validators.
contract.body: Core gameplay contracts should stay fixed where possible and read active configuration PDAs. Civilization consensus changes configuration, not ownership by fiat.
contract.assetProtectionBody: Civilization Law should affect newly generated resources, equipment, and block rules by default. Retroactive effects on existing player assets require Natural Law consensus, stronger prompts, and 90% world-level agreement.

validator.noOwnershipSeizure: Changing ownership of existing player assets

page.title: NiceChunk Resource Rules
page.heading: Public rules for block-to-resource discovery.
page.body: NiceChunk resource rules describe how destroyed blocks become materials. This page exposes the current rule manifest, a deterministic simulator, and the event shape that can later map to verifiable chain logic.
page.conditionValue: Only destroyed stone blocks produce this resource.
page.resultNoReward: {material} is not eligible for this rule.
page.flowTitle: How the test maps to the future contract
page.flow1: The client submits the destroyed block coordinate and material.
page.flow2: The simulator checks whether the material is stone.
page.flow3: If eligible, it returns a deterministic resource reward.
page.flow4: The game hotbar clears non-tool items and records the produced resource.

page.title: NiceChunk Box Resource Atlas
page.heading: Every generated block is a public resource rule.
page.body: NiceChunk maps terrain blocks into scientifically scoped resource foundations. Each block exposes its generation signal, likely elemental composition range, and role in the future mining and forging economy.
page.rawModeBody: Raw resources are mined from generated blocks. They must be smelted into material resources before forging.
page.materialModeBody: Material data defines fuel heat, smelting inputs, artisan requirements, and the forge-ready outputs created from raw resources.
page.modelBody: Ranges are material tendencies, not fixed rewards. Actual resource rolls can later use seed, coordinate, depth, biome, and chain-verified state.
page.empty: No generated blocks match the current filters.

material.title: Raw resources become forgeable materials.
material.body: Raw resources cannot be forged directly. A smelter consumes fuel heat, checks the material recipe, then outputs material resources for item forging.
material.contractBody: This table is a standalone JSON rule source so the game client and future contracts can reference the same smelting requirements.
material.fuelBody: Burnable fuels provide heat tiers. Higher tier recipes require stronger fuel or a hotter furnace source.
material.recipeBody: Browse forge-ready materials by class, inspect their element profile, and review the smelting requirements behind each output.
material.searchLabel: Search materials, classes, elements, or inputs
material.empty: No material recipes match the current filters.
material.visualModelBody: Material thumbnails are generated from the dominant elements in each recipe profile.

toxic_glass.description: Sealed hazardous glass from toxic water, salt, and silica, intended for containment vessels and risk tools.

grass.name: Блок травы
grass.description: Живой верхний почвенный блок: силикатная почва под тонким биологическим слоем, сформированным влажностью, уклоном и плотностью леса.

dirt.name: Почва
dirt.description: Выветренная почва под травой, смешанная с силикатными зернами, глинами, оксидами железа, влагой и органическими остатками.

stone.name: Камень
stone.description: Обычная силикатная порода для холмов, скал, стен пещер и базового геологического материала при добыче.

deepStone.name: Глубинный камень
deepStone.description: Плотная порода нижних пластов с повышенной склонностью к железу и магнию, генерируемая ниже мелкой коры.

sand.name: Песок
sand.description: Кварцевый зернистый осадок, создаваемый правилами пустынь, пляжей, рек, озер и морского дна.

gravel.name: Гравий
gravel.description: Смешанные обломки пород; состав сильно меняется по соседнему рельефу, но остается в основном силикатным.

clay.name: Глина
clay.description: Мелкий гидратированный алюмосиликатный осадок во влажных, пологих местах и слоях рядом с водой.

mud.name: Грязь
mud.description: Почва с высоким содержанием воды и органики, характерная для низких плохо дренированных экосистем с малым доступом кислорода.

dryDirt.name: Сухая почва
dryDirt.description: Высушенная почва с меньшей долей водорода и органики, создаваемая правилами низкой влажности.

saltFlat.name: Солончак
saltFlat.description: Эвапоритовая корка сухого бассейна, где преобладает хлорид натрия с примесями магния, кальция, сульфатов и кислородных солей.

snow.name: Снег
snow.description: Слой замерзших осадков выше снеговой линии или в холодном биоме, возможно с следами минеральной пыли.

ice.name: Лед
ice.description: Твердая фаза воды, выбранная холодными жидкостями и редкими снежными правилами; химически проста, но важна для среды.

frozenSoil.name: Мерзлая почва
frozenSoil.description: Почва типа вечной мерзлоты, смешивающая минеральные зерна, связанный лед и сохраненную органику.

basalt.name: Базальт
basalt.description: Темная мафическая вулканическая порода из быстро остывшей лавы, с большей склонностью к железу, магнию, кальцию и титану.

ash.name: Вулканический пепел
ash.description: Мелкие вулканические частицы из вулканических биомов, похожие на силикатную породу, но более пористые и склонные к сульфатам.

bedrock.name: Коренная порода
bedrock.description: Протокольный фундаментальный слой в основании генерируемых колонн; ведет себя как твердая порода, а не фиксированное химическое месторождение.

water.name: Вода
water.description: Стандартный жидкий блок для океанов, рек, озер и низинных вод, с небольшими различиями растворенных ионов по биому.

swampWater.name: Болотная вода
swampWater.description: Органически богатая стоячая вода из правил болот и влажных земель, со следами растворенного углерода, серы, железа и азота.

toxicWater.name: Токсичная вода
toxicWater.description: Опасная жидкость, выбранная сигналами загрязнения. Основой остается вода, но следовые металлы и сульфиды повышают биологический риск.

lava.name: Лава
lava.description: Расплавленная силикатная жидкость из вулканических сигналов. Состав следует базальтовому расплаву, а не условному фэнтезийному огню.

quicksand.name: Зыбучий песок
quicksand.description: Водонасыщенная песчаная ловушка из пустынной аномальности; в основном кремнезем с водой, снижающей механическую устойчивость.

trunk.name: Ствол
trunk.description: Древесная структурная ткань широколиственных деревьев, состоящая главным образом из целлюлозы, лигнина, воды и минеральных питательных веществ.

leaves.name: Листья
leaves.description: Фотосинтетические блоки кроны; органическая ткань с магниевым хлорофиллом и питательными следовыми металлами.

pineTrunk.name: Сосновый ствол
pineTrunk.description: Смолистая древесина хвойного дерева, создаваемая холодными, высокогорными или вечнозелеными правилами.

pineLeaves.name: Сосновая хвоя
pineLeaves.description: Вечнозеленая хвойная крона с меньшим водообменом и следами минеральных питательных веществ.

deadWood.name: Мертвая древесина
deadWood.description: Высушенная или перегретая древесина с меньшим содержанием водорода и большей долей открытого углерода.

giantRoot.name: Гигантский корень
giantRoot.description: Большая открытая корневая масса из влажных тропических лесов; древесная, но у почвы более влажная и минерализованная.

grassPlant.name: Травянистое растение
grassPlant.description: Малое травянистое растение, выбранное влажными и пологими поверхностными правилами.

dryGrass.name: Сухая трава
dryGrass.description: Высушенный травянистый материал с низковлажных поверхностей, с меньшим водородом и более высокой концентрацией углерода.

bush.name: Куст
bush.description: Древесный кустарниковый слой из правил леса, равнин, гор и тропического леса.

deadBush.name: Сухой куст
deadBush.description: Высохший остаток кустарника, создаваемый пустынными и вулканическими поверхностными условиями.

cactus.name: Кактус
cactus.description: Запасающий воду суккулент из пустынных правил; органический, но с необычно высокой долей связанной воды.

reed.name: Камыш
reed.description: Водное краевое растение, генерируемое рядом с болотами, реками, озерами и влажными землями.

swampGrass.name: Болотная трава
swampGrass.description: Травяной слой влажных земель с органической тканью и осадочным влиянием стоячей воды.

snowBush.name: Снежный куст
snowBush.description: Холодостойкий куст, часто окрашенный снегом или инеем и выбранный горными и снежными правилами.

thorn.name: Колючка
thorn.description: Затвердевший защитный растительный материал сухих или вулканических поверхностей, богатый углеродной структурной тканью.

moss.name: Мох
moss.description: Влажный недревесный биологический мат из влажных лесов, болот и правил рядом с пещерами.

lichen.name: Лишайник
lichen.description: Симбиотическая корка холодных или каменных поверхностей, соединяющая биологическую ткань и минеральный субстрат.

vine.name: Лиана
vine.description: Гибкое углеродистое вьющееся растение, генерируемое во влажных биомах и влажных пещероподобных областях.

glowMycelium.name: Светящийся мицелий
glowMycelium.description: Биолюминесцентный грибной мат из влажных подземных пространств, с усиленными сигналами фосфора и серы.

mushroom.name: Гриб
mushroom.description: Плодовое тело гриба, выбранное редкими случайными результатами влажной растительности.

seaweed.name: Морские водоросли
seaweed.description: Морская растительная биомасса под водой, с следовыми тенденциями натрия, калия, магния и йода.

aquaticPlant.name: Водное растение
aquaticPlant.description: Пресноводная растительность с высоким содержанием воды, поглощающая питательные вещества из осадка.

coral.name: Коралл
coral.description: Структура из карбоната кальция, создаваемая в теплых мелководных условиях.

deadCoral.name: Мертвый коралл
deadCoral.description: Мертвый карбонатно-кальциевый рифовый материал из холодных или загрязненных океанских правил.

shellBed.name: Ракушечный слой
shellBed.description: Накопление фрагментов раковин и карбонатных зерен, создаваемое как редкие пятна побережья и морского дна.

coal.name: Coal
coal.description: Carbon-rich sedimentary fuel generated as low-frequency seams inside deep stone, useful for smelting heat rather than direct forging.

page.title: NiceChunk Elements
page.heading: Elements become resources.
page.body: NiceChunk uses the 118 real chemical elements as the open vocabulary for future resources, forging, material visuals, and verifiable game rules.

resourceGene.title: Resource Gene
resourceGene.body: Each element can become a deterministic material identity for mined drops, block recipes, and future asset metadata.

forgingInput.title: Forging Input
forgingInput.body: Atomic and physical traits map into future processing tiers, heat requirements, risks, and station logic.

visualGene.title: Visual Gene
visualGene.body: Color, metallic, roughness, emission, vein, grain, and crack traits can drive voxel material rendering.

verification.title: Open Verification
verification.body: The table is designed as public rule data so players can inspect how resources are classified before chain commitments.

page.detailsTitle: Click an element to inspect its properties and processing requirements.
page.detailsBody: The requirements below are game-facing placeholders. They define how each element category can later become mined resources, processed materials, and new constructible blocks.
page.summary: {name} is element {atomicNumber}. In NiceChunk it represents a {category} resource that can be discovered from block destruction and later used in block construction rules.
page.constructionHint: {name} can be used as a base {category} component when future recipes define new constructible blocks.

detail.missingZh: Element detail data has not been added yet.
detail.missingEn: Element detail data has not been added yet.

forging.title: Кузнечный стол Nicechunk
forging.chainCodeHint: Этот компактный код хранит компоненты, смещения, размеры, материалы и воксельные вырезы.
forging.chainCodePlaceholder: Вставьте код цепи, чтобы создать редактируемую заготовку.

status.castLocked: Отлитый предмет двигается как одно целое

hero.title: Proof of Short Seed
hero.subtitle: Miners compete to find the shortest valid seed for the current world contour.
hero.body: NiceChunk miners are not searching for meaningless nonces. They search for a Shape Seed that reconstructs the current world contour. More complex contours are harder to describe with a short seed. The first miner to submit a seed that is short enough under the current difficulty solves the epoch and earns the simulated reward.

boundary.title: Frontier Target
boundary.body: The current world contour is canonicalized into target_frontier_root.

context.title: Short Seed Mining
context.body: Miners compete to find a short Shape Seed that reconstructs the current contour.

meaningful.title: First Valid Wins
meaningful.body: The first valid seed accepted for the epoch wins the simulated block reward.

tiny.title: Auto Retarget
tiny.body: Every minute the max_seed_bytes difficulty adjusts to keep blocks near 60 seconds.

grid.title: Current Frontier Target
grid.canvas: Proof of Short Seed world grid

context.title: Neighbor Context
context.note: Generated from unlocked N/E/S/W Chunk Keys in a fixed order.

worldState.title: World State
worldState.seedTargetValue: Harder left: max {max} bytes / RAW {raw} bytes

contour.title: Contour Encoding
contour.body: DecodeShapeSeed v1 supports RAW_EDGES, PATH_RLE, and RECT. RAW_EDGES is the fallback: once max_seed_bytes reaches the raw length, any finite contour is guaranteed solvable.
contour.pastePlaceholder: Paste NSS1|RAW|..., NSS1|PATH|..., or NSS1|RECT|... here.

formulas.title: seed_bytes <= max_seed_bytes
formulas.body: The demo does not prove a seed is globally shortest. It proves the seed reconstructs the current Frontier Root, is canonical, and is short enough under the current max_seed_bytes difficulty.

advanced.title: Commit-reveal for production
advanced.body: Direct reveal is simple for the demo. Production should commit first, then reveal seed and salt after a short delay to prevent another user from copying a valid seed and submitting it first.

transactions.title: SeedBlock Events
transactions.empty: No seed blocks yet. Search and submit a valid seed to solve the first epoch.

status.autoRunning: Auto running: searching and submitting valid seeds.
status.noValidSeed: No valid seed under current max_seed_bytes.
status.rootMismatch: Seed does not reconstruct the current frontier.
status.seedTooLong: Seed is valid but too long for current difficulty.
status.relaxed: No block for 10 minutes. Seed length target relaxed.

messages.locked: This chunk has no unlocked neighbors. Frontier proof requires at least one confirmed neighbor key.
messages.unlocked: This chunk is already unlocked. Its key can now be used by adjacent chunks.
messages.candidate: This chunk can be mined because it touches the current world frontier.
messages.decoded: Decoded {count} frontier edges from the Contour Seed and highlighted the boundary.
messages.invalidSeed: This Contour Seed could not be decoded. Paste a valid nckc_ seed.
messages.copyFailed: Copy failed in this browser context.
